title: Toasted Pan de Campo Slices on a White Plate in Buenos Aires Capital Federal
description: Buenos Aires, Capital Federal: A humble plate of golden-brown pan de campo sits on a worn wooden table, capturing the essence of a traditional Argentine breakfast. The bread, sliced into rustic pieces, shows the distinct char marks of the grill, emphasizing its crisp, toasted texture while suggesting a soft, airy interior. Warm, natural light floods the scene from above, highlighting the uneven edges and flour-dusted surfaces of the slices, which are ready to be paired with local butter or sweet dulce de leche. This intimate framing focuses on the artisanal craft of local baking, turning a simple side dish into a symbol of morning hospitality. The shallow depth of field keeps the crusty bread in sharp focus, while the soft blur of the background hints at the cozy, unhurried atmosphere typical of a neighborhood café. Such small, sensory details are fundamental to understanding the culinary rhythm of Argentine city life and the importance of bread as a centerpiece in daily social gatherings. By documenting these daily rituals, we preserve the intersection of culinary tradition and urban culture that defines the Argentine experience. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
